summary: High‑energy Greek Theatre show with soaring Scarlet>Fire and epic jams
blurb: The first set bursts open with a fiery Shakedown Street and a relaxed Casey Jones, then the second set erupts into a marathon of a 33‑minute Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain, a sprawling Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World, and a soaring encore that lands on Brokedown Palace. Brent’s vocals shine on Little Red Rooster and his surprise keyboard verse adds extra color.
review: On May 23, 1982 the Dead turned the Greek Theatre into a launchpad for one of their most electrifying late‑summer outings. Jerry Garcia kept his voice relaxed on Casey Jones while delivering scorching lead lines throughout the night – especially during the legendary Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain stretch that stretched past thirty minutes. Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar sat front‑and‑center, laying down funky licks and crisp harmonics that helped drive the band’s momentum. Brent Mydland not only added a gritty vocal texture on Little Red Rooster but also surprised the crowd with an impromptu keyboard verse during Ship Of Fools, giving the set a unique twist. The rhythm section was in overdrive: Mickey Hart’s percussive interplay with Bill Kreutzmann produced a pounding, syncopated foundation that powered the long pre‑drums and space excursions. Highlights like Shakedown Street, the Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World jam, The Other One, and an emotionally resonant encore of Brokedown Palace cement this show as a must‑listen snapshot of the band’s 1982 peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the same standout jams (Scarlet>Fire, Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World) and noting no consistent performance flaws; this uniform enthusiasm warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard source with crystal‑clear mix, excellent bass response and minimal technical issues; captures the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Epic 33‑minute Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain jam that showcases group telepathy.
Extended Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World highlights tight interplay and soaring solos.
Brent’s surprise keyboard verse on Ship Of Fools adds a unique, memorable texture.
Pristine soundboard capture delivers crystal‑clear mix, especially the bass and highs.

band_performance.Jerry: Relaxed vocal on Casey Jones and solid, often scorching guitar work; leads the band with fire on Scarlet Begonias > Fire and tight solos throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Front‑of‑house presence defines the mix; funky rhythm, crisp harmonics, and a strong lead that drives the jams.
band_performance.Brent: Vocals stand out on Little Red Rooster; keyboards are "on fire" with organ textures and a surprise verse on Ship Of Fools adding extra color.
band_performance.Mickey: Interacts playfully with the biker onstage; drum presence benefits from clean Meyer system, contributing to the overall power.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers a pounding, tight‑synced drumming that fuels long pre‑drums and high‑energy sections.
